html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 文字隽爽 - Describes the writing style as sharp and elegant.
  • 仿《聊斋》 - Refers to imitations of *Strange Stories from a Chinese Studio*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《萤窗异草》 - A literary work reflecting late Ming and early Qing storytelling traditions, often featuring supernatural elements.
  • 长白浩歌子 - Author's pseudonym, possibly indicating regional or personal identity.
  • 明末清初 - A period of significant historical transition, often a source of inspiration for literary works.
  • 申报馆 - A significant cultural institution in late Qing China, known for publishing newspapers and books.
  • 《笔记小说大观》 - A collection of classical Chinese short stories, indicating the work's place within the broader tradition of Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 《萤窗异草》 - Title of the work.
  • 长白浩歌子 - Author's pseudonym.
  • 申报馆 - Publisher of the work.
  • 《笔记小说大观》 - Collection in which the work is also found.
  • 光绪丁丑(1877) - Year of publication.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 關於作者 - General phrase meaning 'about the author.'
  • 大致上有兩種說法 - Means 'there are roughly two accounts.'
  • 據《八旗藝文編目》 - Means 'according to the *Baqi Wenwen Bianmu*,' a catalog of Manchu literary works.
  • 乾隆時代 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Qianlong.
  • 作者是尹慶蘭 - Means 'the author is Yin Qinglan.'
  • 光緒初年 - Refers to the early years of Emperor Guangxu's reign.
  • 不慕功名 - Means 'not seeking fame or titles.'
  • 耽吟咏 - Means 'enjoying poetry.'
  • 好風雅 - Means 'loving elegance.'
  • 乾隆十九年 - Refers to the 19th year of Emperor Qianlong's reign.
  • 尹繼善督理南河 - Means 'Yin Jishan was in charge of the southern rivers.'
  • 慶蘭與袁枚相識於袁浦署中 - Means 'Qinglan met Yuan Mei in the Yuanpu office.'
  • 詩酒唱和 - Means 'poetry and wine exchanges.'
  • 過從甚密 - Means 'they were very close.'
  • 博學多才 - Means 'broadly learned and talented.'
  • 殿試 - Refers to the final imperial examination.
  • 受稱賞 - Means 'was highly praised.'
  • 終身未仕 - Means 'never held an official position.'
  • 《绚春园诗钞》 - The title of Qinglan's poetry collection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《八旗藝文編目》 - A catalog of Manchu literary works, significant for identifying the authorship of the text.
  • 乾隆時代 - The reign of Emperor Qianlong (1736–1795), a period of cultural and literary flourishing in China.
  • 光緒初年 - The early years of Emperor Guangxu's reign (1875–1908), a time of modernization and reform in China.
  • 尹繼善 - A prominent Manchu statesman and official during the Qing dynasty.
  • 袁枚 - A renowned Qing dynasty poet and essayist, known for his contributions to the *Yangxian School* of poetry.
  • 殿試 - The final stage of the imperial examination system, where candidates were tested on their knowledge of Confucian classics and their ability to compose poetry and prose.

Proper Nouns

  • 《八旗藝文編目》 - A catalog of Manchu literary works.
  • 乾隆時代 - The reign of Emperor Qianlong.
  • 尹慶蘭 - The author of the text, a Manchu poet and scholar.
  • 光緒初年 - The early years of Emperor Guangxu's reign.
  • 尹繼善 - A Manchu statesman and official.
  • 袁枚 - A renowned Qing dynasty poet and essayist.
  • 乾隆十九年 - The 19th year of Emperor Qianlong's reign.
  • 袁浦署 - The office of Yuan Pu, where Qinglan and Yuan Mei met.
  • 《绚春园诗钞》 - Yin Qinglan's poetry collection.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 從慶蘭的经历、志趣和文學素养等情况看 - A phrase meaning 'considering the experiences, interests, and literary cultivation of Cong Qinglan,' used to introduce an evaluation.
  • 有可能成为《萤窗异草》的作者 - Means 'he might have been the author of *Yingchuang Yicao*,' a hypothetical statement about authorship.
  • 没有佐证支持此说 - Means 'there is no evidence to support this claim,' indicating a lack of corroborating proof.
  • 或许从乾隆末到光绪初，《萤窗异草》只是以手写本的形式被人们辗转传阅，影响并不广 - A hypothesis about the manuscript's circulation, meaning 'perhaps from the late Qianlong to the early Guangxu period, *Yingchuang Yicao* was only circulated in handwritten form, with limited influence.'
  • 到光绪初申报馆印行之后，它才风行一时 - Means 'after being published by the Shengbao Press in the early Guangxu period, it became widely popular for a time.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《萤窗异草》 - The title of a classical Chinese collection of short stories.
  • 《小说见闻录》 - A work by Dai Bufan, a modern Chinese scholar, that records his observations and research on classical Chinese fiction.
  • 《聊斋剩稿》 - A manuscript collection related to Pu Songling's *Liaozhai Zhiyi*, a famous collection of classical Chinese ghost stories.
  • 乾隆 - The reign title of Emperor Qianlong of the Qing dynasty.
  • 光绪 - The reign title of Emperor Guangxu of the Qing dynasty.
  • 申报馆 - The Shengbao Press, a prominent publishing house in late Qing and early Republican China.

Proper Nouns

  • 戴不凡 - Dai Bufan, a modern Chinese scholar.
  • 《萤窗异草》 - The title of a classical Chinese collection of short stories.
  • 《小说见闻录》 - A work by Dai Bufan.
  • 《聊斋剩稿》 - A manuscript collection related to Pu Songling's *Liaozhai Zhiyi*.
  • 乾隆 - The reign title of Emperor Qianlong.
  • 光绪 - The reign title of Emperor Guangxu.
  • 申报馆 - The Shengbao Press.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 平步青《霞外捃屑》卷六 - A reference to the sixth volume of the book *Xia Wai Jun Xie* by Ping Buqing, which is a collection of miscellaneous notes.
  • 仿留仙《小翠》 - Imitating Liu Xian's *Xiao Cui*, referring to a style or narrative technique inspired by the work *Xiao Cui*.
  • 同郡 - Means 'from the same prefecture or region.'
  • 戊辰进士 - Indicates that someone passed the imperial examination in the year of the Earth Dragon (a specific year in the Chinese zodiac).
  • 题名碑 - Refers to a stele or monument listing the names of successful candidates in an examination.
  • 馆中黎邱 - Refers to a person named Li Qi who worked in a literary or scholarly institution.
  • 接踵嗣出 - Means 'to follow one another in quick succession.'
  • 殆以齐学裘之《见闻随笔》、《续笔》为劣 - Probably because it considers Qi Xueqiu's *Jian Wen Sui Bi* and *Xu Bi* as inferior.
  • 原题王渔洋评语 - Originally titled with Wang Yuchang's (Wang Fuzhi) comments.
  • 改为随园老人评语 - Changed to comments by Sui Yuan Elder (a reference to Yuan Mei).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 《萤窗异草》 - A collection of strange tales or stories, often fantastical or supernatural.
  • 《子不语》 - A famous collection of ghost stories and anecdotes by Pu Songling, known for its vivid descriptions and moral lessons.
  • 袁枚 - A prominent Qing dynasty writer and poet, known for his literary criticism and the *Zi Bu Yu*.
  • 尹似村 - Another Qing dynasty writer, often mentioned in connection with Pu Songling and his works.
  • 王渔洋 - A reference to Wang Fuzhi, a renowned scholar and critic of the late Ming and early Qing periods.
  • 随园老人 - A reference to Yuan Mei, another famous Qing dynasty writer and critic, known for his literary salon 'Sui Yuan.'
  • 马中骥 - A historical figure, described as a military officer who died while resisting the rebel leader Zhang Xianzhong.
  • 戴敬宸 - A historical figure who passed the imperial examination in the year of the Water Goat (a specific year in the Chinese zodiac).
  • 王立猷 - A historical figure who achieved success in consecutive years in the imperial examinations.
  • 许皋鹤 - A historical figure described as a high-ranking official who died at sea while on a diplomatic mission.
  • 马太史 - A reference to a historian or scholar named Ma.
  • 典楚试 - Refers to the examination held in the province of Chu (modern-day Hubei and Hunan).

Proper Nouns

  • 平步青 - The author of *Xia Wai Jun Xie*.
  • 《霞外捃屑》 - The title of the book by Ping Buqing.
  • 《萤窗异草》 - The title of the collection of stories being discussed.
  • 留仙《小翠》 - The title of the work being imitated.
  • 尹似村 - Another writer mentioned in the text.
  • 吴公畹 - A character in the story.
  • 杜一鸣 - Another character in the story.
  • 马中骥 - A historical figure.
  • 戴敬宸 - A historical figure.
  • 王立猷 - A historical figure.
  • 许皋鹤 - A historical figure.
  • 马太史 - A historical figure.
  • 齐学裘 - The author of *Jian Wen Sui Bi* and *Xu Bi*.
  • 王渔洋 - A reference to Wang Fuzhi.
  • 随园老人 - A reference to Yuan Mei.
  • 黎邱 - A person mentioned as a likely author.
  • 浩歌子 - The pseudonym used by the author of *Zi Bu Yu*.
  • 袁枚 - The author of *Zi Bu Yu*.
  • 尹庆兰 - Another writer mentioned in the text.
  • 《聊斋剩稿》 - A collection of remaining drafts or notes related to *Zi Bu Yu*.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 初编 - Means 'first compilation' or 'first edition.'
  • 序 - Refers to the preface or introduction of a book.
  • 天宝遗迹 - Literally 'remnants of the Tianbao era,' referring to the final years of Emperor Xuanzong's reign, known for its prosperity and later decline.
  • 卜大功 - A name, possibly a person or a title.
  • 金三娘子 - A name, likely referring to a woman.
  • 玉镜夫人 - A title, possibly referring to a noblewoman or a deity.
  • 贾女 - A name, likely referring to a woman.
  • 桃花女子 - Literally 'peach blossom girl,' possibly a metaphor for beauty or a specific person.
  • 红鞋 - Literally 'red shoes,' possibly a metaphor or a specific item.
  • 毒饼 - Literally 'poisonous cake,' likely referring to a harmful or dangerous item.
  • 翠衣国 - Literally 'emerald robe country,' possibly a metaphor for a place or a specific location.
  • 痴婿 - Literally 'foolish son-in-law,' possibly a character or a metaphor.
  • 犬婿 - Literally 'dog son-in-law,' possibly a character or a metaphor.
  • 田凤翘 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 刘天锡 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 桃叶仙 - Literally 'peach leaf fairy,' possibly a character or a metaphor.
  • 冯埙 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 昔昔措措 - A name, possibly referring to a person or a title.
  • 温玉 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 睡姬 - Literally 'sleeping maiden,' possibly a character or a metaphor.
  • 张仙 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 守一女 - A name, likely referring to a woman.
  • 柳青卿 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 珊珊 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 白衣庵 - Literally 'white robe hermitage,' possibly a place or a metaphor.
  • 魂灵 - Literally 'spirit,' referring to a ghost or a soul.
  • 妒祸 - Literally 'jealous disaster,' possibly referring to a negative consequence of jealousy.
  • 李念三 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 訾氏 - A name, likely referring to a person or a family.
  • 假鬼 - Literally 'fake ghost,' possibly referring to a false or imaginary ghost.
  • 银针 - Literally 'silver needle,' possibly a specific item or a metaphor.
  • 赝殃 - Literally 'fake disaster,' possibly referring to a false or imaginary disaster.
  • 落花岛 - Literally 'fallen flower island,' possibly a place or a metaphor.
  • 货郎 - Literally 'peddler,' referring to a person who sells goods.
  • 化豕 - Literally 'transformed into a pig,' possibly referring to a transformation or a metaphor.
  • 缝裳女 - Literally 'sewing skirt girl,' possibly a character or a metaphor.
  • 火龙 - Literally 'fire dragon,' possibly a character or a metaphor.
  • 青眉 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 王秋泉 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 胎异 - Literally 'strange fetus,' possibly referring to a rare or unusual birth.
  • 夏姬 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 郎十八 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 三生梦 - Literally 'three-life dream,' possibly referring to a dream involving multiple lifetimes.
  • 固安尼 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 无常鬼 - Literally 'impermanent ghost,' possibly referring to a ghost or a metaphor.
  • 苏绪 - A name, likely referring to a person.
  • 卫美人 - A title, possibly referring to a noblewoman or a deity.
  • 苦节 - Literally 'bitter virtue,' possibly referring to a person or a metaphor.
  • 狐妪 - Literally 'fox crone,' possibly a character or a metaphor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天宝 - Refers to the final years of Emperor Xuanzong's reign (742-756 AD), a period of prosperity and later decline in Tang Dynasty China.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 萤窗异草初编 - Title of the book, translating to 'Strange Tales of the Lamp Window, First Edition,' suggesting a collection of unusual or fantastical stories.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦吉了 - Could refer to a historical figure or a type of bird.
  • 龙阳君 - Historical figure from the Warring States period.
  • 斗蟋蟀 - Practice of fighting crickets, a popular pastime in ancient China.

Proper Nouns

  • 萤窗异草初编 - Title of the book, translating to 'Strange Tales of the Lamp Window, First Edition.'

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 世俗陋儒 - Refers to 'superficial scholars of the world,' implying those who lack depth in their understanding.
  • 胸无墨渖 - Means 'having no ink in their chest,' a metaphor for lacking knowledge or talent.
  • 立言 - Refers to 'establishing one's words' or 'writing,' often in the context of literary creation.
  • 黜浮华 - Means 'to discard superficiality,' suggesting a rejection of ornate or insubstantial writing.
  • 补救人心、挽回风气 - Means 'to remedy the heart and restore the customs,' indicating an attempt to improve moral and social conditions.
  • 六经廿二史 - Refers to the 'Six Classics and Twenty-Two Histories,' the foundational texts of Chinese literature and history.
  • 圣贤遗训 - Means 'the teachings of sages and virtuous men,' referring to the wisdom of ancient sages.
  • 班班可考 - Means 'can be verified one by one,' emphasizing the reliability and verifiability of these teachings.
  • 迂腐陈言 - Means 'stale and outdated sayings,' suggesting that certain traditional sayings are no longer relevant.
  • 狗尾续貂 - A metaphor for 'adding a dog's tail to a fox's fur,' meaning to add something inferior to something superior.
  • 萤窗异草 - Refers to 'Strange Tales from the Lamp Window,' a collection of stories.
  • 浩歌子 - The author's pseudonym, possibly meaning 'the singer of vast songs.'
  • 尹六公子 - A possible alternative name for the author, suggesting a noble background.
  • 随园老人 - Refers to Yuan Mei, a famous Qing dynasty poet and critic.
  • 酷摹《聊斋》 - Means 'to closely imitate *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio*,' a famous collection of ghost stories by Pu Songling.
  • 新颖处骎骎乎升堂入室 - Means 'novelty that rapidly advances to the inner chambers,' suggesting that the work is innovative and reaches a high level of sophistication.
  • 小说家言 - Refers to 'novelistic discourse,' indicating that the work is more akin to fiction than serious literature.
  • 文人典要 - Means 'essentials for literati,' suggesting the core principles or standards of literary excellence.
  • 消长日、却睡魔 - Means 'to pass the time, to ward off sleep,' indicating that the work is suitable for leisure reading.
  • 贤于近时所刻《见闻随笔》远矣 - Means 'far superior to the *Random Notes on Observations* published recently,' suggesting a comparison with another work.
  • 尊闻阁主人 - Refers to the publisher or editor of the work.
  • 聚珍板 - Refers to a type of movable type printing, known for its high quality.
  • 梅鹤山人 - The author's pseudonym, possibly meaning 'the recluse of the plum and crane.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The Six Classics and Twenty-Two Histories are the foundational texts of Chinese literature and history, including works like the *Book of Songs*, *Book of Documents*, *Book of Changes*, *Book of Rites*, *Book of Music*, and *Spring and Autumn Annals*, as well as historical records from the Han to Qing dynasties.
  • The teachings of Confucius, Mencius, and other sages form the basis of Confucian ethics and philosophy.
  • *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio* is a famous collection of ghost stories by Pu Songling, known for its rich imagination and deep moral insights.
  • *Random Notes on Observations* is another work, likely a collection of anecdotes and observations, which the author compares his work to.
  • This refers to a high-quality printing technique developed during the Qing dynasty, known for its precision and durability.
  • The author's pseudonym suggests a connection to the natural world and a reclusive lifestyle, common among literati in the Qing dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 六经廿二史 - The Six Classics and Twenty-Two Histories.
  • 圣贤遗训 - The teachings of sages and virtuous men.
  • 《萤窗异草》 - *Strange Tales from the Lamp Window*.
  • 浩歌子 - The author's pseudonym.
  • 尹六公子 - A possible alternative name for the author.
  • 随园老人 - Yuan Mei, a famous Qing dynasty poet and critic.
  • 《聊斋》 - *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio*.
  • 《见闻随笔》 - *Random Notes on Observations*.
  • 尊闻阁主人 - The publisher or editor of the work.
  • 聚珍板 - A high-quality printing technique.
  • 梅鹤山人 - The author's pseudonym.

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卷一 - The title of the first chapter or section, indicating the beginning of a larger work.
  • 天寶遗迹 - Remnants of the Tianbao era, a period of Chinese history known for its cultural and economic prosperity under Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 骊山之阴 - The north side of Mount Li, a famous mountain near Xi'an, known for its historical significance.
  • 门忽自裂 - The door suddenly split open, a supernatural or mysterious event.
  • 刍荛者 - A person who cuts grass and wood, referring to a common laborer or rustic person.
  • 惴惴焉 - With fear and hesitation, describing a state of anxiety.
  • 欣然欲往 - Eagerly wanting to go, expressing enthusiasm.
  • 里中喜事而好奇兼饶胆识者 - People from the village who enjoy events, are curious, and have courage and insight, describing a group of adventurous individuals.
  • 携酒食猎具而行 - Carrying food, drink, and hunting tools, indicating a well-prepared expedition.
  • 山径崎岖 - Rough and rugged mountain paths, describing difficult terrain.
  • 荆榛塞路 - Thorny bushes blocking the road, indicating obstacles in the path.
  • 攀附而后上 - Climbing and clinging to reach the top, describing the effort required to ascend.
  • 白石磷磷 - White stones shining brightly, describing the appearance of the stones.
  • 滑腻光泽如有人经行者 - Smooth, glossy, and shiny as if someone had walked on them, suggesting recent activity.
  • 心窃异之 - Secretly feeling strange about it, indicating a sense of wonder or unease.
  • 始达其穴 - Finally reaching the cave, marking the arrival at the destination.
  • 由隙而窥之 - Peering through a crack, describing the act of looking into the cave.
  • 窈杳而深黑 - Deep, dark, and mysterious, describing the appearance of the cave.
  • 怯者 - The timid ones, referring to those who are afraid.
  • 言旋 - Returning home, indicating the act of going back.
  • 勇怯半者 - Those who are half-courageous and half-timid, describing a mixed group of people.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天宝 - The era name of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for its cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 骊山 - Mount Li, a famous mountain near Xi'an, known for its historical significance, including the construction of the Tang Dynasty's imperial palace and the site of the Battle of Yamen.
  • 石洞 - A stone cave, often associated with mystical or sacred places in Chinese culture.
  • 扉 - A door, often used in ancient architecture and literature to symbolize thresholds or transitions.
  • 正统年间 - The reign period of the Ming dynasty's Zhengtong emperor, indicating a specific historical context.
  • 刍荛者 - A common laborer or rustic person, often used to represent the common people in classical literature.
  • 刘瑞五 - A specific individual mentioned in the text, likely a local figure of interest.
  • 猎具 - Hunting tools, indicating a practical preparation for the expedition.
  • 山径 - Mountain paths, often used to describe challenging journeys in classical literature.
  • 荆榛 - Thorny bushes, symbolizing obstacles or difficulties.
  • 攀附 - Climbing and clinging, describing the physical effort required to overcome obstacles.
  • 磷磷 - Shining brightly, often used to describe the appearance of stones or other surfaces.
  • 滑腻光泽 - Smooth, glossy, and shiny, indicating a polished or recently touched surface.

Proper Nouns

  • 天宝遗迹 - The remnants of the Tianbao era, a period of Chinese history known for its cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 骊山 - Mount Li, a famous mountain near Xi'an, known for its historical significance.
  • 石洞 - A stone cave, often associated with mystical or sacred places in Chinese culture.
  • 扉 - A door, often used in ancient architecture and literature to symbolize thresholds or transitions.
  • 正统年间 - The reign period of the Ming dynasty's Zhengtong emperor, indicating a specific historical context.
  • 刘瑞五 - A specific individual mentioned in the text, likely a local figure of interest.
  • 猎具 - Hunting tools, indicating a practical preparation for the expedition.
  • 山径 - Mountain paths, often used to describe challenging journeys in classical literature.
  • 荆榛 - Thorny bushes, symbolizing obstacles or difficulties.
  • 攀附 - Climbing and clinging, describing the physical effort required to overcome obstacles.
  • 磷磷 - Shining brightly, often used to describe the appearance of stones or other surfaces.
  • 滑腻光泽 - Smooth, glossy, and shiny, indicating a polished or recently touched surface.

[10]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 奮臂大呼 - Shouting while raising his arms.
  • 不探此奇，歸有何趣 - Not to explore this wonder; what fun is there in returning home?
  • 篝火燃炬 - Lighting torches with a fire made by surrounding it with a screen of branches.
  • 僅得三入 - Only managed to get three entries.
  • 初入差可骈肩 - At first entering, barely wide enough for shoulders to touch.
  • 渐深而能容驷马 - Gradually deeper, wide enough to accommodate four horses.
  • 两旁皆石壁，洁白晶莹 - On both sides, stone walls, pure white and translucent.
  • 以火烛之 - To illuminate it with fire.
  • 仿佛如有绘画 - As if there are paintings.
  • 境殊不恶，何怯为 - The scenery is not bad; why be afraid?
  • 益深入之，穷其奇 - To delve even deeper, to exhaust its wonders.
  • 曲折数武 - Twisting and turning, several steps.
  • 屏以青玉为之 - Screen made of green jade.
  • 隶书数行，墨迹犹新 - Several lines of clerical script, the ink still fresh.
  • 间与妃子流览其中 - Sometimes with the concubine, stroll and admire within.
  • 几忘其身之匪石也 - Almost forget that their bodies are not stone.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 汉武白云乡 - Metaphorical place of seclusion and peace, often used in poetry to describe an ideal retreat, referring to Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 千秋万岁后 - Phrase indicating a long period of time, often used in historical texts to refer to the future.
  • 良工 - Skilled artisans or craftsmen.

Proper Nouns

  • 独瑞五 - Proper name, likely a historical figure or fictional character.
  • 汉武 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 妃子 - Refers to a concubine of the emperor.

[11]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 始知为明皇所书 - ‘始知’ means ‘only then knew,’ and ‘明皇’ refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang. This phrase indicates that it was only after seeing the signature that one realized the text was written by Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 大可数十楹 - ‘大可’ means ‘can be as large as,’ and ‘楹’ refers to rooms or halls. This phrase indicates that the screen can be as large as several rooms.
  • 中置宝座，仅虚位 - ‘中置’ means ‘placed in the middle,’ ‘宝座’ means ‘throne,’ and ‘仅虚位’ means ‘only an empty seat.’ This phrase describes a throne placed in the middle, but it is empty.

  • 突如作此想，即突然作此事 - Conditional statement meaning 'If one has such a thought, one will act on it.'
  • 冥冥中若无鬼神 - Suggests a sense of moral or spiritual oversight, meaning 'as if there were no divine judgment.'
  • 人即病狂丧心 - Means 'one would lose one's sanity.'
  • 遘此冤狱 - Refers to an unjust legal case, meaning 'suffered this wrongful imprisonment.'
  • 向非某之自白 - Means 'had it not been for the defendant's own admission.'
  • 不几受污名而遭惨死耶 - Means 'would not have been slandered and met a terrible end?'
  • 故折狱者，不可以不慎 - Means 'therefore, judges must be cautious.'
  • 君不游翠衣国几年矣 - Means 'Have you not visited the Land of Green Garments for several years?'
  • 答曰 - Means 'replied, saying.'
  • 丙年离乡，丁年罹罗，今居樊中，岁又三稔，通其首尾计之，已五易春秋矣 - Detailed account of the parrot's past, meaning 'I left my hometown in the year Bing, was captured in the year Ding, and now live in this cage. It has been three years since I was captured, and counting from the beginning to the end, it has been five years.'
  • 颇亦思归否 - Means 'Do you also miss home?'
  • 胡不思？君不知我，我非生而羽者也 - Means 'Why not? You don't know me; I was not born with feathers.'
  • 犹忆昔年为商，贩于湖湘间，贾尝三倍，且颇善言语，恒为人解纷，人无有难之者 - Means 'I still remember the past when I was a merchant, trading in the Hu and Xiang regions, where my profits were tripled, and I was skilled in speech, often helping others resolve disputes, and no one found it difficult.'
  • 某岁仲春，与同伴航海，将谋重利 - Means 'In a certain year of early spring, I sailed with my companions, planning to make a substantial profit.'

  • The sentence '至鏡台之側，無反目之時；繡筐之旁，皆捧足之日' uses parallel structure to emphasize harmony and affection.
  • The sentence '月餘，女病吞酸' introduces a new element of the woman's health.
  • The sentence '大姓夫婦以為異，使黠婢覸之，竊其事以告' describes a plot to uncover the truth.
  • The sentence '竟較之尋常不痴者，殆尤甚焉' makes a comparative statement.
  • The sentence '盡棄兒戲，晨昏定省而外，即坐房中，或藏鉤射覆，或博弈飲酒' describes a shift in their activities.
  • The sentence '靈明已復，積漸能通，久之，竟與女相埒' describes a process of transformation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Conventional opening phrase meaning 'The historian says' or 'The critic remarks.'
  • 瞽者无相，则将伥伥乎奚之 - Metaphorical expression where '瞽者' (blind person) symbolizes someone lacking guidance or vision, and '伥伥乎奚之' (what to do aimlessly) suggests wandering without direction.
  • 人至于雌雄罔辨，痴已甚矣 - Figurative statement indicating extreme confusion or foolishness, where '雌雄罔辨' (cannot distinguish between male and female) implies a state of utter bewilderment.
  • 非有女以相之，又何能脱凡胎而成仙骨耶 - Rhetorical question suggesting that without a woman's assistance, one cannot transcend ordinary existence ('凡胎') to achieve spiritual enlightenment ('仙骨').
  • 女之于此，其柔肠不知几千百转，始肯忍耻含酸，强为此态 - Poetic expression describing a woman's internal struggle and emotional turmoil, where '柔肠' (tender intestines) symbolizes her emotions, and '忍耻含酸' (endure shame and bitterness) reflects her willingness to endure hardship.
  • 金闺女儿，其孰无面目若是哉 - Rhetorical question implying that no respectable woman would behave in such a shameful manner, where '金闺女儿' (daughter of a noble family) represents a woman of high social standing.
  • 余既鉴女之心，益怜女所遇之不幸 - Statement expressing empathy and understanding, where '鉴女之心' (understand the woman's heart) and '怜女所遇之不幸' (pity the woman's unfortunate circumstances) convey the author's compassion.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - Refers to a historian or critic, often used in classical Chinese literature to introduce commentary or analysis.
  • 瞽者 - A blind person, often used metaphorically to represent someone lacking insight or guidance.
  • 雌雄罔辨 - A phrase indicating extreme confusion or lack of discernment, often used to describe a state of mind where one cannot distinguish between right and wrong or good and bad.
  • 凡胎 - Refers to ordinary human beings, often used in contrast to '仙骨' (spiritual enlightenment).
  • 仙骨 - Represents spiritual enlightenment or transcendence, often associated with achieving a higher state of being.
  • 金闺女儿 - Refers to a woman of high social standing, often used to imply respectability and virtue.
  • 含酸 - A term used to describe enduring bitterness or hardship, often associated with emotional pain or suffering.
  • 忍耻 - Refers to enduring shame or humiliation, often used to describe a person's willingness to suffer for a greater cause or to maintain dignity.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure is somewhat elliptical, but it can be understood as 'If a blind person lacks guidance, what will they do aimlessly?'
  • The phrase '人至于' (people to the point of) introduces a state of extreme confusion, and '痴已甚矣' (foolishness has reached its peak) emphasizes the severity of the situation.
  • The rhetorical question structure is clear, but the exact meaning of '脱凡胎而成仙骨' (transcend ordinary existence to achieve spiritual enlightenment) requires careful interpretation.
  • The sentence is complex, with '其柔肠不知几千百转' (her emotions have undergone countless twists and turns) and '始肯忍耻含酸，强为此态' (only then will she endure shame and bitterness, forcing herself into this state) forming a cohesive thought.
  • The rhetorical question structure is clear, but the exact meaning of '无面目' (without face) requires careful interpretation.
  • The sentence is straightforward, but the exact meaning of '鉴女之心' (understand the woman's heart) and '怜女所遇之不幸' (pity the woman's unfortunate circumstances) requires careful interpretation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - A conventional opening phrase meaning 'The historian says,' often used to introduce commentary or historical reflections.
  • 婢之奇，至湘瑟止矣 - Literally 'The marvel of a female servant ends with Xiang Se.' This is a somewhat cryptic expression, likely referring to a specific female servant named Xiang Se whose story is being highlighted.
  • 死生不易其心 - Literally 'Through life and death, her heart does not change.' This phrase emphasizes unwavering loyalty or constancy.
  • 惟以不得事夫子为恨 - Literally 'Only regrets not being able to serve her master.' This suggests a deep sense of loss or regret.
  • 千古侍儿，应推第一 - Literally 'Among all servants throughout history, she should be ranked first.' This is a hyperbolic statement praising the servant.
  • 而天卒报之以花封 - Literally 'But heaven finally rewarded her with a flower seal.' The phrase '花封' (flower seal) is likely a metaphorical or symbolic reward, possibly indicating a posthumous honor or recognition.
  • 志亦毕偿于再世 - Literally 'Her wish was also fulfilled in another life.' This suggests a belief in reincarnation or a continuation of her legacy.
  • 为善降之百祥 - Literally 'Good deeds bring a hundred blessings.' This is a common proverb emphasizing the positive outcomes of virtuous actions.
  • 岂虚语哉 - Literally 'Is it not empty talk?' This rhetorical question affirms the truth of the preceding statement.
  • 独惜主人如李氏，而竟不传其名 - Literally 'Only regret that the owner, like Li, does not pass on his name.' This expresses disappointment that the owner's name is not recorded.
  • 令人弗克识荆 - Literally 'Leaving people unable to recognize him.' This suggests that the lack of a name makes it impossible to identify or remember the person.
  • 是亦天壤间一恨事 - Literally 'This is also a regrettable matter in the world.' This concludes the passage with a sense of loss or dissatisfaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A term used to denote a historian or chronicler, often used in historical texts to introduce commentary.
  • 湘瑟 - Likely refers to a specific female servant named Xiang Se, whose story is being highlighted for her remarkable loyalty and virtue.
  • 千古侍儿 - Refers to female servants throughout history, emphasizing the rarity and significance of Xiang Se's story.
  • 花封 - A metaphorical or symbolic reward, possibly indicating a posthumous honor or recognition.
  • 再世 - Suggests a belief in reincarnation or a continuation of the servant's legacy in another life.

Proper Nouns

  • 外史氏 - A conventional title for a historian or chronicler.
  • 湘瑟 - The name of a specific female servant.
  • 李氏 - The name of the owner, though the exact identity is not specified.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 乃不畏缩，起而曳之使坐曰 — This sequence must be read as a single action: 'then not shrinking back, stood up and pulled him/her to make him/her sit, saying...'
  • 别来无恙？何怨我不相识耶？ — This is a compound question, where the first part is a greeting and the second part is a rhetorical question.
  • 乃鬼狐来取汝命耳 — This statement is a dramatic declaration, emphasizing the supernatural threat.
  • 尚竟坦然，惟以目抵女面，睫毛几刺其颊 — This sequence describes Shang's calm demeanor and his physical interaction with the woman.
  • 施朱太赤，敷粉太白，古人不我欺矣 — This is a poetic statement, where the woman criticizes the excessive use of makeup.
  • 人皆具眼，一目了然。奈何令眸子逼人至此？ — This is a rhetorical question, emphasizing the woman's frustration with Shang's intense gaze.
  • 两相谐谑，欢然止宿 — This describes the playful interaction between Shang and the woman, leading to them staying overnight.
  • 明旦，始别去 — This marks the end of their interaction, with them parting ways the next morning.
  • 君实献之后身，妾即桃叶，虽鬼而已仙矣。情缘未断，因以相投。 — This is a complex statement where the woman reveals her identity and the nature of their relationship.
  • 君能与妾久处，当令君寿。慎勿泄之他人，使造言者疑我也。 — This is a conditional promise, with a warning not to reveal the secret.
  • 尚喜得丽质，生死俱所不辞，遂不惧其鬼，而反昵之。 — This describes Shang's reaction to the woman's beauty and his decision to become close to her despite her being a ghost.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 雖至契密友之前，未嘗微露其概 - Concessive construction meaning 'even though she was in the presence of close and intimate friends, she never revealed even a hint of it.'
  • 女昼去夕来，日益缱绻 - Temporal construction meaning 'the woman would leave during the day and come at night, and their affection grew increasingly tender.'
  • 即有客乘夜过访，而女之至也无形，女之避也无迹 - Conditional construction meaning 'even if there were visitors who came at night, the woman's arrival was invisible, and her departure left no trace.'
  • 私语喁喁，属垣者褎如充耳；笑声吃吃，窥户者阒其无人 - Parallel construction meaning 'their whispers were so soft that those who were listening would pretend not to hear; their laughter was so light that those who were watching would think there was no one there.'
  • 其行踪诡秘如此，尚益信其真仙 - Resultative construction meaning 'her movements were so mysterious that Shang believed even more strongly that she was a true fairy.'
  • 无何，而尚疾矣 - Temporal construction meaning 'not long after, Shang fell ill.'
  • 女时来问视，汤药必亲，俨然伉俪，而疾亦小愈 - Temporal construction meaning 'the woman would often come to visit, personally preparing the medicine, and they seemed like a married couple, and his illness slightly improved.'
  • 独向之痴情不断，每值其来，欲与之合，女怍然辞曰：“妾误君几危，犹忍以床笫相惑耶？” - Temporal construction meaning 'but his infatuation with her remained unbroken, and every time she came, he wanted to be with her, but she blushed and said, 'I almost caused you to die, how could I still tempt you with intimacy?''
  • 尚不听，强之共寝，明日而疾又大作 - Temporal construction meaning 'Shang did not listen, forcing her to sleep with him, and the next day his illness worsened.'
  • 女叹曰，“予害夫子，将不可复为人矣。” - Direct speech construction meaning 'the woman sighed and said, 'I have harmed Master, and I will no longer be human.''
  • 尚正色曰：“即使今为卿死，已愈于徒生，何憾为？” - Direct speech construction meaning 'Shang said sternly, 'Even if I die for you now, it is better than living in vain, what is there to regret?''
  • 女终引为己过 - Resultative construction meaning 'the woman finally took it as her own fault.'
  • 幸尚之艰于远视，遂匿迹韬声，虽日侍尚之左右，而不使一见之 - Resultative construction meaning 'fortunately, Shang had difficulty seeing far away, so she remained hidden and silent, even though she served him day and night, he never saw her.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昔昔未及答 - 昔昔 refers to a past time, and 未及答 means had not yet responded. Together, it suggests a delayed response or reaction.
  • 措措早已入室 - 措措 is likely a name or nickname, and 已经入室 means had already entered the room, indicating a prompt action.
  • 姊对生人倾吐底里 - 姊 means sister, 对生人 means to a stranger, and 倾吐底里 means to pour out one's innermost thoughts. This phrase describes a sister revealing her deepest secrets to a stranger.
  • 得毋使客笑我 - 得毋 means how can it be that, and 使客笑我 means make the guest laugh at me. Together, it expresses concern about being laughed at by the guest.
  • 婢子太不谙事 - 婢子 means maid or servant, and 太不谙事 means too inexperienced or too naive. This phrase criticizes the maid for her lack of understanding.
  • 郎君实有厚福 - 郎君 means young master or young man, and 实有厚福 means truly has great fortune. This phrase praises the young man for his good fortune.
  • 将藉以返故乡 - 将藉以 means will use, and 返故乡 means return to one's hometown. This phrase indicates that the young man will use his fortune to return home.
  • 何讳焉 - 何讳 means why hide, and 焉 is a particle that can be translated as it or that. This phrase asks why something should be hidden.
  • 毒龙悦色 - 毒龙 means poisonous dragon, and 悦色 means pleased with the appearance. This phrase describes a dragon being pleased by someone's appearance.
  • 其体纯阳 - 其体 means its body, and 纯阳 means purely yang or purely masculine. This phrase describes the dragon's body as purely masculine.
  • 知蚕神之美 - 知 means knows, 蚕神 means silkworm deity, and 之美 means its beauty. This phrase indicates that the dragon knows the beauty of the silkworm deity.
  • 数数嬲之 - 数数 means repeatedly, and 嬲之 means torment it. This phrase describes the dragon repeatedly tormenting the silkworm deity.
  • 食其种类殆尽 - 食 means eat, 其种类 means its kind, and 殆尽 means almost entirely. This phrase indicates that the dragon has almost entirely consumed the silkworm deity's kind.
  • 蚕神惧 - 蚕神 means silkworm deity, and 惧 means fear. This phrase describes the silkworm deity's fear.
  • 不得已而亲诣海藏 - 不得已 means unwillingly, 而 is a conjunction meaning and, 亲诣 means personally visit, and 海藏 means ocean treasure or ocean depths. This phrase describes the silkworm deity's reluctant visit to the ocean depths.
  • 自献其身 - 自献 means offer oneself, and 其身 means its body. This phrase describes the silkworm deity offering its body.
  • 缘与龙为欢 - 缘 means because, 与 means with, 龙 means dragon, and 为欢 means to have fun or to enjoy. This phrase describes the silkworm deity's enjoyment with the dragon.
  • 不甚主人世事 - 不甚 means not very, 主 means to control, and 人世事 means worldly affairs. This phrase indicates that the silkworm deity does not control worldly affairs.
  • 蛊毒亦不甚验 - 蛊毒 means poisonous insects or toxicity, and 亦不甚验 means are also not very effective. This phrase indicates that the toxicity is not very effective.
  • 据卿言 - 据 means according to, and 卿 means you (a respectful term). This phrase means according to what you say.
  • 神有雌雄 - 神 means deity, and 有雌雄 means have male and female. This phrase describes deities as having male and female aspects.
  • 当成配偶 - 当 means should, 成 means form, and 配偶 means spouse. This phrase suggests that deities should form spouses.
  • 今毒龙恣意贪淫 - 今 means now, 毒龙 means poisonous dragon, 恣意 means arbitrarily, and 贪淫 means lustful. This phrase describes the dragon's current arbitrary lust.
  • 神之雄者能无怒耶 - 神之雄者 means the male deity, 能 means can, 无 means not, and 怒 means anger. This phrase asks if the male deity can remain angry.
  • 郎诚解人 - 郎 means you (a respectful term), 诚 means truly, and 解人 means understanding person. This phrase praises the young man for being truly understanding.
  • 妾辈所虑者正在此 - 妾辈 means we, 所虑者 means what we worry about, and 正在此 means is right here. This phrase indicates that their worry is precisely this.
  • 彼一妖 - 彼 means that, 一 means one, and 妖 means demon. This phrase refers to a demon.
  • 不敢与龙为仇 - 不敢 means dare not, 与 means with, 龙 means dragon, and 为仇 means make enemies. This phrase indicates that the demon dare not make enemies with the dragon.
  • 将以妾等泄其欲 - 将 means will, 以 means with, 妾等 means us, and 泄其欲 means satisfy its desire. This phrase indicates that the demon will satisfy its desire with them.
  • 深惧其毒 - 深惧 means deeply fear, and 其毒 means its poison. This phrase indicates a deep fear of the demon's poison.
  • 故避之不遑 - 故 means therefore, 避之 means avoid it, and 不遑 means without time or without pause. This phrase indicates that they avoid it without pause.
  • 然则奈何 - 然则 means then, and 奈何 means what to do. This phrase asks what to do in this situation.
  • 昨夕渡君 - 昨夕 means last night, 渡 means cross, and 君 means you (a respectful term). This phrase indicates that they crossed you last night.
  • 良非无意 - 良 means indeed, 非 means not, and 无意 means without intention. This phrase indicates that it was indeed not without intention.
  • 妾等实人身 - 妾等 means we, 实 means indeed, and 人身 means human body. This phrase indicates that they indeed have human bodies.
  • 犹可奉君箕帚 - 犹 means still, 可 means can, 奉 means serve, and 箕帚 means broom and dustpan (metaphorically, household chores). This phrase indicates that they can still serve you with household chores.
  • 今拟相从而归 - 今 means now, 拟 means plan, 相从 means follow each other, and 归 means return. This phrase indicates that they plan to follow you back.
  • 为君立业 - 为 means for, 君 means you (a respectful term), and 立业 means establish a career or build a foundation. This phrase indicates that they will establish a career for you.
  • 苗疆非善地 - 苗疆 means Miao region, and 非善地 means not a good place. This phrase indicates that the Miao region is not a good place.
  • 愿君毋思深入也 - 愿 means hope, 君 means you (a respectful term), 毋 means do not, 思 means think, and 深入 means go deep. This phrase hopes that you do not think about going deep into the Miao region.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無何而天已昧爽 - 无何 is an adverb meaning 'soon' or 'shortly after', and 天已昧爽 means 'the sky had already grown dim and bright' (dawn). Together, this phrase indicates the passage of time, specifically the early morning.
  • 可不可，一言而决断 - 可不可 is a rhetorical device asking 'whether it is permissible or not', and 一言而决断 means 'decided by a single word'. This phrase emphasizes the importance of decisive action.
  • 妾辈亦将束装他往 - 妾辈 refers to the speaker and others in a humble way, and 束装他往 means 'to pack up and go elsewhere'. This phrase indicates preparation for departure.
  • 君勿过于踌躇，翻以误我 - 过于踌躇 means 'to hesitate too much', and 翻以误我 means 'to end up confusing me'. This phrase advises against overthinking and suggests that hesitation could lead to confusion.
  • 遂欣然曰：“诺。” - 欣然 means 'with pleasure' or 'gladly', and 诺 is a formal affirmative response. This phrase shows the speaker's agreement and willingness.
  • 二女皆大笑曰：“君盍少俟，容妾整顿，即可行。” - 盍少俟 means 'why not wait a little', and 容妾整顿 means 'let me get ready'. This phrase indicates a request for a short delay.
  • 状类雄苗 - 状类 means 'resembling in appearance', and 雄苗 means 'male child'. This phrase describes the appearance of the two women after they change clothes.
  • 顾谓邹曰：“持此去吃着不尽，无事作汗漫游矣。” - 吃着不尽 means 'to have more than enough to eat', and 无事作汗漫游 means 'to wander aimlessly without a care'. This phrase suggests a life of abundance and freedom.
  • 仍循故道，以筏渡水，而翼邹登陟，其疾如飞 - 循故道 means 'to follow the old path', 以筏渡水 means 'to cross the water with a raft', and 翼邹登陟 means 'to help Zou climb'. This phrase describes the journey and the assistance provided.
  • 回顾而太息曰：“神纵知之，亦莫予追也已！” - 回顾 means 'to look back', 太息 means 'to sigh deeply', and 神纵知之，亦莫予追也已 means 'even if the gods knew, they would not be able to catch us'. This phrase expresses a sense of relief and escape.
  • 是夕，宿于旅舍，遂成欢好 - 是夕 means 'that night', 宿于旅舍 means 'stayed at an inn', and 遂成欢好 means 'they became intimate'. This phrase describes the night they spent together.
  • 二女皆柔媚，邹益乐之 - 柔媚 means 'gentle and charming', and 益乐之 means 'found it even more enjoyable'. This phrase describes the qualities of the two women and Zou's enjoyment.
  • 数日，经一苗寨，措措与昔昔耳语，俱笑不能仰 - 经一苗寨 means 'passed through a Miao village', 耳语 means 'whispered', and 俱笑不能仰 means 'both laughed so hard they couldn't look up'. This phrase describes a moment of amusement.
  • 邹询之，昔昔曰：“君勿言，今夕当有乐地，且以之泄吾幽愤。” - 邹询之 means 'Zou asked them', 君勿言 means 'don't say anything', 今夕当有乐地 means 'tonight will be a place of joy', and 且以之泄吾幽愤 means 'and we will use it to vent our hidden anger'. This phrase indicates a secretive and joyful plan.
  • 遂行 - 遂行 means 'they continued on their way'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝廉 - Title for a scholar-official in the Han dynasty, indicating high moral and academic standing.
  • 玉娘子 - A woman of high virtue and beauty, often used in literature to denote a virtuous and beloved woman.
  • 岳帝 - The Lord of Mount Hua, a deity associated with justice and retribution in Chinese mythology.
  • 冥司 - The underworld or the realm of the dead, governed by various deities and officials.
  • 扶柩而过者 - A funeral procession, where the coffin is carried by mourners.
  • 红毹 - A red carpet or cloth, often used in funerals to cover the coffin.
  • 缟素 - White clothing, traditionally worn during mourning.
  • 弱息 - A term for a daughter, often used to express affection and tenderness.
  • 既笄而殀 - A young woman who died before reaching adulthood; '笄' refers to the hairpin ceremony marking a girl's coming of age.
  • 发牒 - To issue a decree or order, often used in the context of the underworld.
  • 往生他处 - To be reborn in another place, often implying a better afterlife.

  • 孝廉喜，治筵相款 - The preparation of a feast reflects the social customs of classical Chinese society.
  • 命焚其榇于郭 - The burning of the coffin at the city gate reflects the cultural practices surrounding death and the supernatural.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 春闱 - The imperial examinations were a crucial part of the Chinese civil service system, determining one's social and political status.

  • 邑宰 - County magistrates were local officials responsible for administration and justice.
  • 新蔡 - New Cai was a county in Henan Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 秦州牧 - Qin Prefecture was an important administrative region during the Han Dynasty.
  • 卓异 - Outstanding performance was recognized and rewarded in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 安庆守 - Anqing was a significant city in Anhui Province, and its governor held a high-ranking position.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 玉謂之曰 - Formal way of saying 'Jade said to him,' where '玉' (Jade) is likely a nickname or title.

  • 君岂忘扬州之梦耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Do you not forget the dream of Yangzhou?' where '之梦' (the dream) refers to a past experience or aspiration.
  • 时玉已两索皆男 - 'At that time, Jade had already two sons,' with '两索' (two requests) likely referring to two pregnancies.
  • 孝廉雅不欲 - 'The scholar-official was naturally reluctant,' where '雅' (naturally) indicates a habitual or intrinsic attitude.
  • 因玉固请，乃从之 - 'Because Jade insisted firmly, he finally agreed,' with '固请' (insisted firmly) and '乃从之' (finally agreed) showing the sequence of events.
  • 为留十日 - 'He stayed for ten days,' where '为留' (stayed) indicates a deliberate decision.
  • 大索于瘦马家 - 'He searched extensively in the homes of thin horses,' with '瘦马' (thin horses) likely a metaphor for impoverished families or women.
  • 濒行矣 - 'As he was about to leave,' where '濒' (on the verge of) emphasizes the immediacy of the departure.

  • 玉适从太夫人游平山堂归 - 'Jade had just returned from a trip with his mother to Ping Shan Hall,' where '适' (just) indicates the timing.
  • 泫然曰 - 'Said tearfully,' where '泫然' (tearfully) conveys emotional distress.
  • 妹何一寒至此 - 'Why are you so cold?' where '一寒' (so cold) is a metaphor for poverty or hardship.
  • 星眸炯炯然 - 'Her eyes were bright and shining,' where '星眸' (star-like eyes) is a poetic description.
  • 玉亲为盥栉 - 'Jade personally washed and combed her,' where '盥栉' (washed and combed) indicates care and attention.
  • 眉目焕然 - 'Her eyebrows and eyes looked refreshed,' where '焕然' (refreshed) conveys a sense of renewal.
  • 询其年，果十五 - 'He asked her age, and she was indeed fifteen,' where '果' (indeed) confirms the information.
  • 因仍以柔字之 - 'He still called her 'Rou,' where '仍' (still) indicates continuity.
  • 不欲没其旧也 - 'He did not want to erase her old name,' where '没其旧' (erase her old name) conveys a sense of respect for her past.
  • 及夜定情 - 'At night, they consummated their relationship,' where '定情' (consummated their relationship) indicates a formal commitment.
  • 孝廉试其啼笑 - 'The scholar-official tested her laughter and tears,' where '试' (tested) implies a deliberate observation.
  • 宛然一柔 - 'She was exactly like Rou,' where '宛然' (exactly like) emphasizes similarity.
  • 益狂喜，谓玉言不谬 - 'He became even more ecstatic, saying that Jade's words were not wrong,' where '益狂喜' (even more ecstatic) conveys intense joy.
  • 履数任后 - 'After serving in several positions,' where '履数任' (serving in several positions) indicates a career progression.
  • 玉虽主中馈 - 'Jade was in charge of the kitchen,' where '主中馈' (in charge of the kitchen) indicates a domestic role.
  • 而视柔若匹敌 - 'But he treated Rou as an equal,' where '匹敌' (equal) conveys a sense of parity.

  • 期年，柔亦诞一子 - 'After a year, Rou also gave birth to a son,' where '诞' (gave birth) indicates the birth of a child.

  • 遂不出仕 - 'He then did not take up office,' where '不出仕' (did not take up office) indicates a decision to retire.
  • 遨游于温柔之乡以终老 - 'He wandered in the land of comfort until his old age,' where '温柔之乡' (land of comfort) conveys a sense of ease and contentment.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Indicates the beginning of a commentary or personal reflection, often used in classical texts.
  • 取其德也 - Means 'to take its virtue,' referring to the moral qualities of jade.
  • 殆无愧乎 - Means 'probably not ashamed,' suggesting a high degree of moral integrity.
  • 不妒不淫 - Refers to the virtues of not being jealous and not being promiscuous.
  • 女让当夕 - Means 'the woman let her turn come,' implying she was willing to wait her turn.

  • 其贤如此 - Means 'her virtue is like this,' emphasizing her moral character.

  • 碎者复全，分者复合 - Means 'the broken ones are restored, the separated ones reunite,' symbolizing the restoration of harmony and unity.
  • 温其如玉 - Means 'as warm as jade,' using a metaphor to describe her virtue.
  • 不致徒悼于重泉 - Means 'not to grieve in vain in the netherworld,' suggesting that her virtue would prevent unnecessary sorrow in the afterlife.
  • 柔娘，独无可取 - Means 'Rou Niang, alone has no redeeming qualities,' criticizing her behavior.
  • 惟愿为女一节，聊可解嘲 - Means 'only wishing to be as virtuous as the woman, one can somewhat alleviate mockery,' suggesting that even minor virtue can be commendable.
  • 非温玉之淑，又乌能附骥尾以传也哉 - Means 'Without the virtue of Wen Yu, how could one attach oneself to the tail of a swift horse and be known?' using a metaphor to suggest that without Wen Yu's virtue, one cannot achieve fame or recognition.
  • 随园老人曰 - Indicates the beginning of another commentator's reflection.
  • 人谓似聊斋《莲香传》 - Means 'People say it is similar to the 'Lian Xiang' in Liaozhai,' referring to a story in the collection *Liaozhai Zhiyi*.
  • 非鬼狐之迹略同 - Means 'Not because the traces of ghosts and foxes are roughly the same,' suggesting that the similarity is not due to supernatural elements.

  • 事奇文奇 - Means 'The story is strange and the writing is strange,' highlighting the unusual nature of the narrative.
  • 安在莲香后不可复有温玉 - Means 'Why can't there be Wen Yu after Lian Xiang?' questioning the uniqueness of the story.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A term often used in classical Chinese literature to denote a commentator or historian.

  • 随园老人 - Refers to Yuan Mei, a prominent poet and scholar of the Qing dynasty, known for his literary works and gardens.
  • 聊斋 - Refers to *Liaozhai Zhiyi*, a famous collection of short stories by Pu Songling, known for its supernatural and fantastical elements.
  • 莲香传 - A specific story in *Liaozhai Zhiyi* that is being compared to the current narrative.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 旋有赤色如匹練，直达中楹，则一丽人含怒自外入 - This sentence introduces a supernatural event and the appearance of a woman, requiring careful reading to maintain the sequence of events.
  • 视之，乃其妾也，鸾鹤犹翔其侧 - This clause identifies the woman and describes the presence of supernatural beings, which must be read together.
  • 贵官大惭，回顾少年，已化为石卵，大仅盈掬 - This clause describes the nobleman's reaction and the transformation of a youth, requiring a clear sequence of actions.
  • 妾掇之，回嗔为笑曰 - This clause describes the concubine's actions and words, which must be read as a continuous action.
  • 本意引公入胜，不期鸾迟鹤速，以致惑于邪妖，究亦足以释公之惑矣 - This clause explains the concubine's original intention and the unexpected outcome, requiring a clear causal relationship.
  • 今将返妾故居，不得再充下陈，慎勿过悲 - This clause describes the concubine's decision and advice, which must be read as a single thought.
  • 此公之可人所化，剖之当获宝玉，不致以杀风景怨妾 - This clause explains the origin of the stone egg and the potential reward, requiring a clear explanation.
  • 言讫，溘然而逝 - This clause describes the concubine's death, which must be read as a sudden and final event.
  • 知其仙，厚加葬具，题之曰：“睡姬之墓。” - This clause describes the nobleman's actions after the concubine's death, requiring a clear sequence of actions.
  • 破其璞，得明视一枚，朱眼霜毫，天然工巧 - This clause describes the discovery of the precious gem, requiring a clear sequence of actions.
  • 因宝之，佩以终身，斯须未尝去焉 - This clause describes the nobleman's lasting bond with the gem, requiring a clear sequence of actions.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不使尘中贵人深历瑶天胜境 - Poetic expression meaning 'not allowing the noble person of the world to deeply experience the heavenly paradise'.
  • 重门击柝犹防暴客 - Phrase meaning 'even with multiple gates and guards, one must still be wary of violent intruders'.

  • 不俟姬之导引，然后如入华胥 - Phrase meaning 'without the concubine's guidance, one can still enter the realm of the Hua Xiu (a mythical land of dreams)'.
  • 人患不善睡、不暇睡耳 - Phrase meaning 'the problem is not knowing how to sleep or not having time to sleep'.
  • 果如姬之高枕，纵不居芙蓉城，亦平生第一快事 - Phrase meaning 'if one can sleep as comfortably as the concubine, even if one does not live in the city of lotus flowers, it would still be the greatest pleasure of life'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - Common term used by authors to refer to themselves when writing historical or biographical works, implying a role as a chronicler or historian.
  • 瑶天胜境 - Refers to a heavenly paradise or a place of great beauty and tranquility.
  • 重门击柝 - Refers to the security measures of a well-protected residence, including multiple gates and guards.
  • 世外仙居 - Refers to a hermit's abode, often associated with seclusion and spiritual purity.
  • 黑甜乡 - Metaphor for deep sleep, often used in classical literature.
  • 华胥 - Refers to a mythical land of dreams or a utopian realm.
  • 高枕 - Metaphor for a comfortable and peaceful sleep.
  • 芙蓉城 - Refers to a city of lotus flowers, often used symbolically to represent a place of beauty and tranquility.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '究是妾之狡狯，不使尘中贵人深历瑶天胜境' is elliptical and can be read as 'considering the cunning of the concubine, she does not allow the noble person of the world to deeply experience the heavenly paradise'.
  • The sentence '不然，重门击柝犹防暴客，岂世外仙居竟任狂童出入哉' is a rhetorical question, emphasizing the contrast between the security of a well-protected residence and the openness of a hermit's abode.
  • The sentence '至于黑甜乡中，原有乐趣，固不俟姬之导引，然后如入华胥' is a compound structure, linking the inherent pleasure of sleep with the idea that one does not need the concubine's guidance to enter a dreamlike state.
  • The sentence '人患不善睡、不暇睡耳' is a concise statement, highlighting the common problems of not knowing how to sleep or not having time to sleep.
  • The sentence '果如姬之高枕，纵不居芙蓉城，亦平生第一快事' is a conditional statement, suggesting that the greatest pleasure of life can be achieved through comfortable sleep, even without living in a beautiful place.

  • 独居寂寞，情何以堪 - Phrase meaning 'Living alone in solitude, how can one endure it?'; translated as 'Living alone in solitude, how can one bear it?'
  • 且予稔知兄尚无嗣，宗祀亦重事，不可固辞 - Phrase meaning 'Moreover, I know well that you still have no heir, and the continuation of the ancestral line is a serious matter, which cannot be firmly declined'; translated as 'Moreover, I know well that you still have no heir, and the continuation of the ancestral line is a serious matter, which cannot be firmly declined.'
  • 太史究以朝命为嫌 - Phrase meaning 'The Grand Historian still objects to the imperial command'; translated as 'The Grand Historian still has reservations about the imperial command.'
  • 太史意乃定，复以姻娅礼拜孙夫妇，咸大悦，呼之以许郎，盖从女家称之也 - Phrase meaning 'The Grand Historian's mind was then decided, and he again bowed to Sun and his wife in the manner of a relative. Both were very pleased, and called him 'Xu Lang,' which is the term used by the family of the woman'; translated as 'The Grand Historian's mind was then decided, and he again bowed to Sun and his wife in the manner of a relative. Both were very pleased, and called him 'Xu Lang,' which is the term used by the family of the woman.'

  • 许郎 - Term of endearment used by the family of the woman, likely referring to the Grand Historian's new brother-in-law.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 良时已届 - Formal expression meaning 'the auspicious time has arrived,' often used in wedding or ceremonial contexts.
  • 群婢拥女出堂 - Literally 'the maidservants escort the bride out of the hall,' a traditional description of the bride being led out for the wedding ceremony.
  • 璎络重遮 - Refers to the bride being adorned with heavy jewelry and ornaments.
  • 绾金步摇 - Describes the bride wearing a golden hairpin with a decorative tassel.
  • 衣翠羽氅 - Means 'wearing a green feathered cloak,' indicating the bride's elaborate attire.
  • 妆束若天仙 - Poetic expression meaning 'dressed as if she were a celestial being,' emphasizing the bride's beauty and elegance.
  • 交拜已 - Means 'after the mutual bowing,' referring to the traditional wedding ritual where the couple bows to each other.
  • 送入青庐 - Refers to the bride being escorted into the bridal chamber, a symbolic act in traditional Chinese weddings.
  • 孙自张筵款诸眷 - Means 'Sun himself arranges the feast to entertain the guests,' indicating the host's hospitality.
  • 太史与女对坐 - Means 'the historian sits opposite the bride,' referring to the historian (likely a guest or official) sitting with the bride.
  • 饮合欢之杯 - Refers to drinking from a cup symbolizing marital bliss.
  • 绾同结之心 - Means 'tying together hearts,' a metaphor for the couple's union.
  • 阿姊以郎君清华贵客 - Means 'the elder sister considers the groom a distinguished and noble guest.'
  • 染翰凤池 - Refers to writing poetry, with '凤池' (Fengchi) being a metaphor for the imperial court or a place of literary excellence.
  • 催妆无诗 - Means 'there is no poem to accompany the bride's makeup,' implying that the groom should compose a poem for the occasion.
  • 合卺必当有句 - Means 'there must be a poem for the union of the cups,' referring to the traditional wedding ritual where the couple shares a cup of wine.
  • 愿赐数行，以志今夕之胜 - Means 'I hope you will grant a few lines to commemorate this night's splendor,' a polite request for the groom to compose a poem.
  • 别却黄麻驾彩虹 - Poetic line meaning 'farewell to the yellow hemp and ride the rainbow,' likely a metaphor for leaving behind the mundane and ascending to a higher state.
  • 乘槎今入斗牛宫 - Refers to 'boarding a raft and entering the constellation of Dou and Niu,' a metaphor for entering a celestial or divine realm.
  • 不须更把支机赠 - Means 'there is no need to give more gifts of support,' implying that the groom's presence and poetry are sufficient.
  • 自有皇华傲粉红 - Means 'there is already the imperial elegance that surpasses the pink,' a poetic way of praising the bride's beauty and the groom's status.
  • 腕未及停，又一婢自外入曰 - Means 'before his wrist could rest, another maid entered from outside,' indicating the rapid succession of events.
  • 大姊有诗申贺 - Means 'the elder sister has a poem to celebrate,' referring to another guest offering a congratulatory poem.
  • 使星耀自九重天 - Poetic line meaning 'the star envoy shines from the nine heavens,' likely a metaphor for the groom's noble status.
  • 金屋新看结好缘 - Means 'a new alliance is seen in the golden house,' referring to the newly formed marital bond.
  • 寄语刘郎须得意 - Means 'send a message to Liu Lang to be pleased,' a reference to the story of Liu Xiang and the poem 'Chang'e Flying to the Moon.'
  • 桃源还胜杏花前 - Means 'the Peach Blossom Spring is even more beautiful than before the Apricot Flower,' a poetic way of praising the bride's beauty and the groom's happiness.

  • 白衣庵 - A temple or shrine, possibly named after a white robe or a white-clad figure.

  • 因谄其父以重金，罗而致之 - Means 'he flattered the father with a large sum of money and brought Ya Jiu to him.'
  • 亚九遂为优于滇 - Means 'Ya Jiu became a renowned performer in Yunnan.'
  • 声容并妙，名擅梨园 - Means 'his voice and appearance were both excellent, and he was famous in the theater.'
  • 每一讴，座客争为缠头 - Means 'every time he sang, the audience competed to give him gifts.'
  • 诸伶咸愧其不及 - Means 'all the other performers were ashamed of their inability to match him.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 道者一目即笑曰 - Classical phrase meaning 'the Daoist immediately smiled and said,' indicating a personification of the Daoist as a wise and playful figure.
  • 忠言逆耳，反以见疑 - Common idiom meaning 'sincere advice is hard to hear and is often met with suspicion.'
  • 今急而求我，无能为矣 - Phrase meaning 'now that you are in a hurry and seek me, I cannot help you,' indicating a refusal due to the urgency of the situation.
  • 诚难固却 - Phrase meaning 'it is truly difficult to refuse,' suggesting a reluctant acceptance.
  • 俟发长尺余，当相见也 - Phrase meaning 'wait until your hair grows a foot long, and we will meet again,' indicating a promise to meet at a future time.
  • 亚九益顿首 - Phrase meaning 'Ya Jiu bowed deeply,' indicating a gesture of respect or gratitude.
  • 与若有良缘，诚难固却 - Phrase meaning 'if there is a good fate, it is truly difficult to refuse,' suggesting a belief in destiny.
  • 乃引与疾行 - Phrase meaning 'he then led him to walk quickly,' indicating a rapid movement.
  • 至一土窟，使入 - Phrase meaning 'to a cave, and made him enter,' indicating a specific location and action.
  • 此中食物皆备，可自制炊 - Phrase meaning 'all the food is prepared here, and you can cook it yourself,' indicating self-sufficiency.
  • 因俯躬而入 - Phrase meaning 'he then bowed and entered,' indicating a respectful and humble action.
  • 其中绝宽大，约可数楹 - Phrase meaning 'the inside is extremely spacious, about the size of several rooms,' indicating the size and comfort of the space.
  • 床榻俱以土筑成，衾褥亦具 - Phrase meaning 'the beds and mats are all made of earth, and the quilts and pillows are also provided,' indicating the basic but sufficient living conditions.
  • 旁有侧户，窥之，米麦堆积 - Phrase meaning 'there is a side door, and looking through it, you can see rice and wheat piled up,' indicating the availability of food supplies.

  • 道者亦不复来，亚九心益安 - Phrase meaning 'the Daoist did not come again, and Ya Jiu's heart became even more at ease,' indicating a sense of security and peace.
  • 惟冀其发速长 - Phrase meaning 'he only hoped that his hair would grow quickly,' indicating a specific desire or goal.
  • 年余，几盈尺 - Phrase meaning 'after more than a year, it almost reached a foot in length,' indicating the passage of time and the growth of hair.
  • 窟后故有池，因勤沐之 - Phrase meaning 'there was a pool behind the cave, and he bathed frequently,' indicating a routine of self-care.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Common phrase in classical Chinese texts to introduce a commentary or reflection, often by a historian or commentator.
  • 風聲鶴唳，草木皆兵 - Chinese idiom meaning to be so frightened that every sound or sight seems threatening, often used to describe extreme paranoia.
  • 人自倉惶，鬼何能為祟哉 - Rhetorical question suggesting that if people are already in a state of panic, ghosts cannot cause any additional harm.
  • 世之狡者，又故借幽冥劣相以吓嗤嗤之氓 - Describes how cunning people might use the image of evil spirits to frighten the ignorant or gullible.
  • 吾不知真鬼聞之，其亦揶揄否耶 - Rhetorical question expressing doubt about whether real ghosts would mock such behavior.
  • 猶憶京師某巷，有鬼夜深辄出 - Reminiscence about a specific alley in the capital city where a ghost was said to appear at night.
  • 宵行者遭之，每遺棄衣物 - Describes how people walking at night would encounter the ghost and often leave behind their clothes.
  • 其首如栲栳，紙條飛鳴，周身皆白毫，約寸許 - Describes the ghost's appearance, with a head like a winnowing basket, paper strips fluttering, and white hair about an inch long.
  • 朱其目，赤其口，形状可怖 - Describes the ghost's red eyes and red mouth, making it appear terrifying.
  • 王已沉酣，莫惧，反谩骂曰 - Describes the patrolman Wang, who, drunk, did not fear the ghost and instead insulted it.
  • 若鬼耶應避人，汝反逐人耶 - Rhetorical question challenging the ghost, suggesting that if it is a ghost, it should avoid people, not chase them.
  • 鬼聞之折身却走，如辟易 - Describes the ghost turning away and retreating, as if repelled.
  • 王察其有异，疾趋而前，捽之以力，鬼亦仆 - Describes the patrolman noticing something unusual, rushing forward, and physically overpowering the ghost.
  • 主审知为人，剥其面，褫其革，径抱以归 - Describes the patrolman discovering that the 'ghost' was actually a person, removing its mask and skin, and carrying it back home.
  • 灯下视之，则羊裘一袭，乱毛如蝟，面具乃以汲水器为之，涂以殊墨败楮，乱粘而已 - Describes the 'ghost' as a sheepskin cloak with messy fur, a mask made from a water bucket, and a face painted with unusual ink and old paper, all crudely attached.
  • 明日传视，见者俱大笑 - Describes how the next day, when the 'ghost' was shown to others, everyone laughed.
  • 王至今犹衣其裘，但未稔其人雌雄 - Describes the patrolman still wearing the sheepskin cloak, but not knowing the gender of the person it belonged to.
